Izabel, I read the recovery plan. It looks strong, but I want to test it. Are you and your team fixing all of this yourselves?
No. That is the point. We are having each problem handled by a specialist. We are having the firmware rewritten by an external security firm, not in-house.
Good. And the forecast that caused the excess stock?
We are getting an independent analyst to rebuild the model. I do not want the same people who built the bad forecast to grade their own work.
What about the process itself? A launch like this should never slip through again.
I have had the whole release checklist overhauled. Nobody signs off on a shipment now until quality has certified it, and any missed date is escalated to me the same day.
And when the team cannot fix something?
Then we bring in a third party rather than let it drift. Every item has one accountable owner who must get the fix done. That is how a setback becomes a turnaround.
We got an independent analyst to rebuild the forecast.
Nobody signs off on a shipment until quality certifies it.
We bring in a third party rather than let it drift.

Izabel, it is Marcus, your operations partner, with a progress note on the Halo recovery before the review. The turnaround is under way. We are having the firmware rewritten by an external security firm this week, and we will get the whole system retested before a single unit ships. On the process side, I have had the release checklist overhauled from scratch, because it was the old checklist that let the defect through. Here is the change that matters most: every fix now has a named owner. Nobody signs off on a shipment until quality has certified it, and any missed date is escalated to me the same day. Where we cannot rectify a gap in-house, we bring in a third party rather than let it drift. I am not going to pretend it was painless, but we are getting the whole organization up to speed fast. Call me before the review and I will walk you through the owners.

Good morning, Izabel. It is Sofia, your operations lead, with the remediation update you asked for. Let me be precise. The firmware is the priority, so we are having it rewritten by specialists, and I am getting an independent analyst to rebuild the forecast model, because the unrealistic forecast is what caused the excess stock. On supply, I have had the contract reworked so that a late shipment is flagged in writing and escalated the same day. The biggest change is ownership. In the past, a fault was noticed and nobody took ownership; now each item has one accountable owner who must get the fix done and report every Thursday. Remediation is not open-ended; every task has a date. If we have the right work done by the right people, this setback becomes a turnaround. I will bring the full owner list on Thursday.
